short: Adding up the adjustments from several small batches before applying one update to the model.
definition: Gradient accumulation adds up the suggested adjustments from several small batches of data before applying a single update to the model, imitating a larger batch than fits in memory at once. This lets limited hardware gain the stability benefits of large batches without holding all the data at the same time. It trades a few extra passes through the data for lower peak memory use.
